title: Dispatch item C of brief lead-231: catalog schema bd-decoupling (6 message types) to shopsystem-messaging
description: request_bugfix dispatch to shopsystem-messaging covering item C of brief 001 (inter-shop messaging encapsulation). Bundles 6 scenarios from features/messaging/08..13-*.gherkin pinning the invariant 'minimal valid <type> can be constructed with no required bd-related field' for each of the 6 currently-implemented catalog schemas: AssignScenarios, RequestBugfix, RequestMaintenance, Clarify, WorkDone, MechanismObservation. Pre-state empirically verified by reading repos/shopsystem-messaging/src/catalog/schemas.py: 5 of 6 schemas (RequestMaintenance, AssignScenarios, RequestBugfix, Clarify, WorkDone) define NO bd_* field at all — their scenarios are regression pins against unpinned existing behavior. MechanismObservation.bd_ref is required (Field pattern '^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*-[a-z0-9]+$', line 178); its scenario tightens by demanding bd_ref become optional / removed / renamed to provenance_ref (Implementer's pick consistent with invariant). request_scenario_register and request_shop_card are deferred per the brief — no schemas exist for them in catalog/schemas.py. Bundled (not split) because the 6 scenarios share one invariant; the Implementer needs to see surface coherence. Parent brief: briefs/001-inter-shop-messaging-encapsulation.md (lead-231).

title: Dispatch item A of brief lead-231: shop-msg CLI surface (pending enumeration + read inbox + --bc filter) to shopsystem-messaging
description: assign_scenarios dispatch to shopsystem-messaging covering item A of brief 001 (inter-shop messaging encapsulation). Carries 7 scenarios from features/messaging/01..07-*.gherkin pinning: list-pending enumeration (empty + populated + processed-exclusion + lead-side --bc filter) and read inbox (happy path + missing work_id + schema-invalid). Pre-state empirically verified: shop-msg today exposes only respond/send/read with read having only outbox subcommand; no pending/list/next subcommand exists; no --bc filter exists. New capability => assign_scenarios. Parent brief: briefs/001-inter-shop-messaging-encapsulation.md (lead-231).

title: Brief: inter-shop messaging encapsulation (shop-msg as sole surface; self-contained messages)
description: Lead-shop brief authored from mechanism_observation 'shopsystem-templates-kin' against lead-kq0. Names two invariants (shop-msg is the sole inter-shop messaging surface; inter-shop messages are self-contained), the bd-decoupling corollary, and five scope items (A: shop-msg CLI surface; B: templates rewrite; C: catalog schema decoupling from bd; D: template-language decoupling from bd; E: spec edits to sections 4 and 5). Sequencing: A+C+E parallel; B+D wait on lead-kq0 (PDR-001) closing. Scenario authoring deferred until Architect verifies BC pre-state and picks vehicles. Artifact: briefs/001-inter-shop-messaging-encapsulation.md.
notes:
Intent gaps resolved 2026-05-12 by user-driver:
1) Tests carve-out from invariant 1: messaging-BC's own tests may inspect storage; consumer-BC tests use the CLI.
2) Item E (spec edits): drop any filename convention (incl. <work_id>-<response_type>.yaml) from §4. Spec proper stays conceptual — names flows and CLI ops only; storage shape is shop-msg internal.
3) Item C (catalog audit): keep full audit of all 8 message types; do NOT narrow to 'verify others don't regress' — that presupposes the other 7 are clean, which has not been verified empirically.

---
2026-05-12 (PO subagent) — Authored 15 scenarios for items A and C of brief 001.
Location: features/messaging/01..15-*.gherkin. Target BC: shopsystem-messaging (tagged @bc:shopsystem-messaging).

A — shop-msg CLI surface (7 scenarios, 01..07):
  01 list-pending empty mailbox
  02 list-pending reports work_id and message_type
  03 list-pending excludes processed (inbox has matching outbox)
  04 list-pending lead-side --bc filter
  05 read inbox by work_id
  06 read inbox missing work_id fails
  07 read inbox schema-invalid content fails

C — Catalog schema bd-decoupling (8 scenarios, 08..15, one per message type):
  08 assign_scenarios   09 request_bugfix   10 request_maintenance
  11 request_scenario_register   12 request_shop_card   13 clarify
  14 work_done   15 mechanism_observation
Each C scenario pins the invariant ("minimal valid instance has no required bd-related field") without picking a realization (optional vs. removed vs. renamed-to-provenance_ref) — per brief, Architect's call.

Pre-state hints noted while authoring (Architect verifies):
- shop-msg cli.py exposes only `read outbox` today; no `read inbox`, no `pending`/`list` subcommand. Reinforces brief's discriminator hint that A is assign_scenarios shape.
- Of the 8 catalog schemas in the brief's enumeration, only MechanismObservation has a required bd_ref field (catalog/schemas.py line 178). Quick grep shows no other bd_* fields in the schemas file. Reinforces brief's discriminator hint that C is request_bugfix shape — tightening MechanismObservation, with the other 7 scenarios serving as regression-pins.

Open question for Architect: scenarios 11 (request_scenario_register) and 12 (request_shop_card) name two of the eight message types the brief enumerates, but catalog/schemas.py defines only 6 Pydantic classes (RequestMaintenance, AssignScenarios, RequestBugfix, Clarify, WorkDone, MechanismObservation). The other two may live elsewhere in the catalog package, or may not yet exist. The scenarios are phrased "the catalog schema for message_type X" to leave room either way — but if the brief's enumeration is itself stale, please flag back.

Did NOT dispatch. Architect picks vehicles and composes shop-msg send.

---
2026-05-12 (PO subagent, scope correction) — Empirical reality reconciled.

Scenarios 11 and 12 (request_scenario_register, request_shop_card) DELETED. Those Pydantic schemas do not exist in repos/shopsystem-messaging/src/catalog/schemas.py, and prototype-1 P3 issues 6mk and r7u closed-as-deferred 2026-05-10 — no open lead-shop bead schedules authoring. The two message types are deferred per findings/from-prototype-1.md finding 1.

Remaining scenarios renumbered contiguously (13→11, 14→12, 15→13). Final layout:
  features/messaging/01..07: item A (CLI surface) — unchanged
  features/messaging/08..10: item C — assign_scenarios, request_bugfix, request_maintenance
  features/messaging/11..13: item C — clarify, work_done, mechanism_observation

Final scenario count: 13 (was 15). Item C now covers the 6 currently-implemented catalog schemas.

Brief 001 item C amended in step:
- Audit scoped to 6 currently-implemented message types.
- request_scenario_register and request_shop_card called out as deferred, with explicit pointer to findings/from-prototype-1.md finding 1.
- Decoupling invariant carries forward: future authoring of those two schemas honors the invariant from the start.
- request_scenario_register's schema authoring is named to naturally ride with lead-otu (per-BC scenario register surface) — since that message type depends on what a register IS. request_shop_card awaits its own driver.

Architect: the Open Question in the prior PO note (whether the brief's enumeration was stale) is now resolved — the enumeration WAS stale relative to empirical schema reality; the brief is corrected. No follow-up flag back needed on that point.

title: Restructure lead-po and lead-architect templates to be role-complete (PDR-001)
description:
Lead-shop work item tracking the dispatch to shopsystem-templates BC for the
role-complete restructure of lead-po.md and lead-architect.md.

Intent driver: PDR-001 (pdr/001-role-templates-role-complete.md).

The PO subagent authors scenarios in this repo's features/templates/ pinning
what role-completeness means against §3.2's activity catalogue. The Architect
subagent then verifies the templates BC's pre-state empirically, applies the
message-type discriminator, and dispatches via shop-msg send to the
shopsystem-templates BC.

This beads ID is the work_id that flows outward in the inter-shop message
per §6.
notes:
Architect dispatched assign_scenarios to shopsystem-templates BC on 2026-05-12.

Empirical pre-state verification: ran `shop-templates show lead-po` and
`shop-templates show lead-architect`. Confirmed: neither template names the
§3.2 activities the role-complete restructure requires (Interview stakeholder,
Maintain product brief, Write PDR, Reconcile scenario registers, Write ADRs,
Maintain structurizr, BC decomposition). The capability genuinely does not
exist today — discriminator Q1 → assign_scenarios.

Scenarios 09-16 all passed well-formed Gherkin sufficiency check. CLI computed
hashes and added @bc/@scenario_hash tags. Message deposited at
repos/shopsystem-templates/inbox/lead-kq0.yaml.

Scenario hashes (CLI-computed):
- 09 f1ac9534c1d58318
- 10 dddd6c3b2eed7e45
- 11 9a9421ad59ee5d67
- 12 e522f7393dfcd1c1
- 13 a481db51463526d2
- 14 0aea22a97e63d4f8
- 15 5ccb3fb1122f9341
- 16 a6b3e510821aba52

PO's 4 open questions accepted as-is by Architect with rationale (BC has no
existing 'guidance pending' convention; depth-3+ is a meaningful step; both
short and full §3.2 phrasing appear across scenarios; PDR-001 permits
guidance-pending marker for activities lacking sufficiency-check shape).

Awaiting work_done from BC. Close lead-kq0 on reconciliation.
close_reason: work_done received via shop-msg read outbox (status: complete). All 8 scenario hashes match dispatch one-to-one (f1ac9534c1d58318, dddd6c3b2eed7e45, 9a9421ad59ee5d67, e522f7393dfcd1c1, a481db51463526d2, 0aea22a97e63d4f8, 5ccb3fb1122f9341, a6b3e510821aba52). Post-state spot-check via shop-templates show lead-po / show lead-architect confirms substantive restructure: all 5 PO + 8 Architect §3.2 activities now present as ### subsections with one-sentence-plus guidance, where at dispatch they were absent. BC reports BDD 53/0 and probes considered+dismissed. No scenario register exists under features/templates/ yet (raw .gherkin files only); no ad-hoc register created per scope.

Backfill scenarios for shopsystem-scenarios BC

title: shop-msg not on default PATH — requires venv activation for cold dispatches
description:
Surfaced during lead-kq0 reconciliation on 2026-05-12.

shop-msg is installed at /workspaces/shopsystem-product/.venv/bin/shop-msg but the venv is not activated by default. Any agent dispatched cold (fresh subagent, new shell) must 'source .venv/bin/activate' first or invoke the absolute path; otherwise 'shop-msg ...' fails with command-not-found. The lead-architect's reconciliation pass hit this and worked around it.

What needs to be done: pick one of (a) ensure venv activation is automatic for any lead-shop session (devcontainer postCreate / shell rc), (b) install shop-msg outside the venv so it is on PATH unconditionally, (c) update subagent templates and CLAUDE.md to always invoke the absolute path or activate first. (c) is the lowest-touch / no install changes; (a) or (b) are more systemic. Choose during pickup.

title: Scenario register surface for templates BC (lead-architect activity currently degenerate)
description:
Surfaced during lead-kq0 reconciliation on 2026-05-12.

The post-lead-kq0 lead-architect template names 'Reconcile scenario registers against assigned work' as a §3.2 activity. No register surface exists under features/templates/ — only the raw .gherkin files (01-16). The activity is structurally degenerate for the templates BC until a register is introduced; the role names a responsibility the mechanism doesn't yet realize.

What needs to be done: decide between (a) introduce a per-BC scenario register surface in the lead shop carrying authored / dispatched / signed-off status (designed once for all BCs, not just templates), or (b) accept reconciliation against the templates BC as degenerate by design and document why. If (a), the design likely interacts with the lead-shop drain workflow noted as deferred in findings/from-mechanism-observation-v1.md §6.
